title: Graph Object
description: The graph that describes the volumetric model. Possible node types: "BoxSDF", "CylinderSDF", "SphereSDF", "TorusSDF", "PlaneSDF", "ReferencedGeometrySDF", "ReferencedCurveLength", "ReferencedCurveCoords", "ReferencedFaceCoords", "GradientVector", "InvertDensity", "PerlinNoiseScalar", "VoronoiNoiseScalar", "Shell", "ConstantScalar", "ConstantColor", "ImageSamplerScalar", "ImageSamplerVector", "ImageSamplerColor", "3DImageSamplerScalar", "SphereCoords", "TorusCoords", "CylinderCoords", "HomogenousTransformCoords", "TransformCoords", "AxisBasedDeformCoords ", "TwistCoords", "ControlPointMapScalarToScalar", "ControlPointMapScalarToColor", "FalloffMapping", "VectorToColor", "CombineScalarsToVector", "CombineScalarsToColor", "SplitVectorToScalars", "SplitColorToScalars", "LengthOfVector", "NormalizeVector", "ExternalColor", "FunctionScalarToScalar", "FunctionVectorToColor", "FunctionVectorToVector", "FunctionVectorToScalar", "BinaryOperatorColor", "BinaryOperatorVector", "BinaryOperatorScalar", "MultiplyColor", "MultiplyVector", "MultiplyScalar"

methods.name: addNode
methods.description: Add a new node to the graph. Node names are unique, attempting to add two nodes with the same name produces an error.

methods.name: canEvaluateGraph
methods.description: Check if all the channels in the graph can be evaluated and in a good state.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getNode
methods.description: Get node with the given name.

methods.name: getNodeInputPinConnector
methods.description: Get an upstream connection to the node's input pin.

methods.name: getNodeOutputPinConnectors
methods.description: Get an array of downstream connections from the node's output pin.

methods.name: getOutputNode
methods.description: Get one of the special graph output nodes. Every graph has one or more, the cannot be created or added. The final output pins of the graph nodes should be connected to these to make a useful graph.

properties.name: allGraphConnectors
properties.description: Get all the connectors in the graph.

properties.name: allNodes
properties.description: Get all the nodes in the graph, including the output nodes.

properties.name: allPossibleNodeTypes
properties.description: Get all the possible node types that can be used as the nodeType parameter for addNode.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Volumetric Custom Feature API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to create a Volumetric Custom Feature using the API for graph creation.

To run the sample script, have a document open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will create a component with a box by sketching then extruding that sketch. It will then use that box as a boundary body and create a Volumetric Custom Feature.

The script will the create a gyroid lattice using the Volumetric Model API with the appropriate Graphs, Nodes and Connections for a minimal example. Finally, the script will convert that Volumetric Model to Mesh using the API and the VolumetricModelToMeshFeature.

addNode.title: Graph.addNode Method
addNode.description: Add a new node to the graph. Node names are unique, attempting to add two nodes with the same name produces an error.
addNode.return_description: The new node if it could be added, null otherwise.

parameters.name: nodeType
parameters.description: The node type string, one of the types listed in the documentation.

related_samples.name: Volumetric Custom Feature API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to create a Volumetric Custom Feature using the API for graph creation.

To run the sample script, have a document open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will create a component with a box by sketching then extruding that sketch. It will then use that box as a boundary body and create a Volumetric Custom Feature.

The script will the create a gyroid lattice using the Volumetric Model API with the appropriate Graphs, Nodes and Connections for a minimal example. Finally, the script will convert that Volumetric Model to Mesh using the API and the VolumetricModelToMeshFeature.

canEvaluateGraph.title: Graph.canEvaluateGraph Method
canEvaluateGraph.description: Check if all the channels in the graph can be evaluated and in a good state.
canEvaluateGraph.return_description: True if this graph can be evaluated, false otherwise.

classType.title: Graph.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

parameters.name: outputNode
parameters.description: The node where the connection starts.

parameters.name: outputPinIndex
parameters.description: The index of the output pin on the start node.

parameters.name: inputNode
parameters.description: The node where the connection ends.

parameters.name: inputPinIndex
parameters.description: The index of the input pin on the end node.

related_samples.name: Volumetric Custom Feature API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to create a Volumetric Custom Feature using the API for graph creation.

To run the sample script, have a document open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will create a component with a box by sketching then extruding that sketch. It will then use that box as a boundary body and create a Volumetric Custom Feature.

The script will the create a gyroid lattice using the Volumetric Model API with the appropriate Graphs, Nodes and Connections for a minimal example. Finally, the script will convert that Volumetric Model to Mesh using the API and the VolumetricModelToMeshFeature.

parameters.name: outputNode
parameters.description: The node where the connection starts.

parameters.name: outputPinIndex
parameters.description: The index of the output pin on the start node.

parameters.name: inputNode
parameters.description: The node where the connection ends.

parameters.name: inputPinIndex
parameters.description: The index of the input pin on the end node.

getNode.title: Graph.getNode Method
getNode.description: Get node with the given name.
getNode.return_description: The node if found, null otherwise.

getNodeInputPinConnector.title: Graph.getNodeInputPinConnector Method
getNodeInputPinConnector.description: Get an upstream connection to the node's input pin.
getNodeInputPinConnector.return_description: An array of GraphConnector objects, one for each connection to another node.

parameters.name: inputPinIndex
parameters.description: The index of the input pin of the node.

getNodeOutputPinConnectors.title: Graph.getNodeOutputPinConnectors Method
getNodeOutputPinConnectors.description: Get an array of downstream connections from the node's output pin.
getNodeOutputPinConnectors.return_description: An array of GraphConnector objects, one for each connection to another node.

parameters.name: outputPinIndex
parameters.description: The index of the output pin of the node.

getOutputNode.title: Graph.getOutputNode Method
getOutputNode.description: Get one of the special graph output nodes. Every graph has one or more, the cannot be created or added. The final output pins of the graph nodes should be connected to these to make a useful graph.
getOutputNode.return_description: The output node if present in the graph, null otherwise.

parameters.name: outputNodeType
parameters.description: The type of the output graph node. Not every graph has every type; the primary graph has all four ouput node types, the cell graph only has OutputDensity.

related_samples.name: Volumetric Custom Feature API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to create a Volumetric Custom Feature using the API for graph creation.

To run the sample script, have a document open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will create a component with a box by sketching then extruding that sketch. It will then use that box as a boundary body and create a Volumetric Custom Feature.

The script will the create a gyroid lattice using the Volumetric Model API with the appropriate Graphs, Nodes and Connections for a minimal example. Finally, the script will convert that Volumetric Model to Mesh using the API and the VolumetricModelToMeshFeature.
